questionText: Has your organization mapped its value chain?
sectorTags: Financial services, Oil & Gas, Coal, General, Aviation, Capital goods, Transport OEMS - EPM, Chemicals, Metals & mining, Transport services, Steel, Agricultural commodities, Transport OEMS, Energy utilities & power generators, Cement, Food, beverage & tobacco, Paper & forestry, Real estate, Construction
simpleHelp: This question allows data users to understand the extent to which your organization has visibility into different parts of its value chain, to facilitate activities such as effective risk management, traceability, and supplier engagement. Additionally, responses to this question will help data users interpret the supplier coverage reported across the rest of your responses regarding action in your value chain.

options: Yes, we have mapped or are currently in the process of mapping our value chain
No, but we plan to do so within the next two years
No, and we do not plan to do so within the next two years
optionsKo: 예, 당사 매핑된 또는 프로세스 매핑 당사의 가치사슬
, 그러나 당사 계획 그렇게 내 향후 2개 연도
, 및 당사 아님 계획 그렇게 내 향후 2개 연도

columns.text: Value chain stages covered in mapping
Highest supplier tier known but not mapped
options: All supplier tiers known have been mapped
columns.textKo: 파악했으나 매핑하지 않은 최상위 공급업체 단계

columns.text: Description of mapping process and coverage
columns.textKo: 매핑 프로세스 및 적용 범위 설명

columns.text: Primary reason for not mapping your upstream value chain or any value chain stages
options: Lack of internal resources, capabilities, or expertise (e.g., due to organization size)
Judged to be unimportant or not relevant
columns.textKo: 주된 이유 에 대한 아님 매핑 귀사의 업스트림 가치사슬 또는 모든 가치사슬

columns.text: Explain why your organization has not mapped its upstream value chain or any value chain stages
columns.textKo: 설명 왜 귀사의 조직 아님 매핑된 그것의 업스트림 가치사슬 또는 모든 가치사슬

guidance.text:
General 
Organizations may have multiple value chains related to different commodities or products. This question requests organizations to consider their upstream (e.g. suppliers') and downstream (e.g. customers') activities across all their value chains as an organization-wide value chain.
When identifying your organization's suppliers, consider all entities who provide or sell raw materials, processed materials, and/or finished products or services to your organization.
If disclosing on forests, note that "value chain mapping" (the process of identifying stakeholders within your value chain, including where they operate and how they relate to each other) is related to but distinct from "traceability" (the ability to follow products through stages of the value chain). You can report details of your commodity traceability levels in question 8.8.
Value chain stages covered in mapping (column 2) 
If you are a financial services discloser, select "Portfolio" when referring to the companies and/or activities within your portfolio that you lend to, invest in, or insure. Select "Upstream value chain" when referring to your operational activities. This question asks whether you are aware of the different parts of your portfolio, where the companies operate and how they relate to each other.
Highest supplier tier mapped (column 3) 
This column only appears if you select "Upstream value chain" in column 2 "Value chain stages covered in mapping".
Select the highest supplier tier you have mapped or are currently mapping relating to any product or commodity across your portfolio. If your mapping exercise only includes some of your suppliers within a tier, you should still select this tier. For example, if you have mapped 100% of tier 1 suppliers, 50% of tier 2, and 0% of tier 3 and beyond, then select "Tier 2". You will then be able to indicate the percentage for each tier mapped in the following columns.
"Highest" in the context of supplier tiers refers to the furthest tier away from your direct suppliers.
Highest supplier tier known but not mapped (column 4) 
Select the highest supplier tier in your upstream value chain that you are aware of or that you presume exists. This is applicable even if you have not mapped your upstream value chain, as you could still be aware that you have direct suppliers at a minimum if you purchase products from other organizations.
Supplier tiers known but not mapped could also be based on your mapped suppliers' products, activities, sectors, etc.
For example, an organization has mapped their tier 1 suppliers. They determine that one of their tier 1 suppliers does not produce the raw materials used in the product they buy. Instead, the supplier procures it from another company. In this case, although the organization has not yet mapped suppliers beyond tier 1, they are aware that tier 2 suppliers must exist, at a minimum. Therefore, the organization would select "Tier 1 suppliers" in column 3 "Highest supplier tier mapped" and "Tier 2 suppliers" in this column.
If you select "Upstream value chain" in column 2 "Value chain stages covered in mapping", the option "All supplier tiers known have been mapped" will be presented:
Select this option if you have mapped or are in the process of mapping all known supplier tiers for your "Upstream value chain".
Only select this option if you do not have additional tiers in your upstream value chains beyond those reported as mapped.
Smallholder inclusion in mapping (column 6) 
This column only appears if you are disclosing on Forests and "Upstream value chain" is selected in column 2 "Value chain stages covered in mapping". If you are disclosing on multiple themes, you are only required to report information about smallholders included in your forests-related value chain mapping.
Select "Smallholders relevant and included" if your organization sources from smallholders and they are included in your upstream value chain mapping.
Smallholders that you engage with but do not source from can also be relevant to your value chain if your engagement proves to be mutually beneficial to the smallholders and your organization (i.e., through providing benefits and positive incentives to smallholders while enabling your organization to fulfil its supply chain commitments and secure adequate volumes of responsibly produced commodities).
If you consider smallholders that you do not source from relevant to your value chain and include them in your value chain mapping, select "Smallholders relevant and included".
If they are relevant but not included in your value chain mapping, and neither are any of the smallholders you source from, select "Smallholders relevant but not included".
Description of mapping process and coverage (column 7) 
Provide a description of your organization's mapping process, including, for example:
The type of information that has been collected;
The tools and methods used; and
The coverage of your mapping (i.e., whether it is full or partial).
guidance.textKo: 이 섹션은 해당 문항에서 CDP가 요청하는 작성 내용을 설명합니다. 위 응답방법, 열 구조, 드롭다운 선택지를 기준으로 보고연도와 조직 범위에 맞는 정보를 입력하십시오.

guidance.text: Organizations have knowledge of the actors in their value chain and the relations between them, to effectively identify, assess and manage their environmental dependencies, impacts, risks, and opportunities.
guidance.textKo: 이 섹션은 문항 응답에 필요한 추가 작성 기준을 설명합니다. 응답방법과 선택지를 함께 확인하여 작성하십시오.

guidance.text:
Downstream value chain : the activities, sites, resources, relationships, and stakeholders which receive products and/or services from your organization. The downstream value chain varies depending on the nature of the business but may include customers, distributors, logistics providers, and packaging suppliers (adapted from ESRS, 2023 ). 
Smallholder: small-scale agricultural or forest products producers that are distinct from larger-scale producers found in similar contexts by virtue of many or all of the following characteristics: 
High degree of dependence on family labor. 
Profits accrue primarily to the farm/forest owner and their family. 
The farm/forest provides a primary source of income livelihood for the smallholder. 
Production units have a relatively small land footprint (relative to the range of production unit sizes for the given commodity and region). 
Household resources are allocated to both food crops and cash crops. 
Relatively low use of agricultural inputs and generally low productivity and yields. 
Significant economic constraints, such as lack of capital assets and low access to finance. 
Significant information constraints, including lack of technical knowledge and low access to market information (adapted from  AFi, 2024 ). 
Supplier: an entity upstream from the reporting organization that provides or sells raw materials, processed materials, finished products or services to the reporting organization (adapted from GHG, 2013 ; ESRS, 2023 )
Tier 1 supplier (also referred to as "direct supplier"): a supplier that provides or sells products directly to the reporting organization ( GHG Protocol, 2013 ). 
Tier 2, 3, or 4+ supplier: a
supplier that provides or sells products to the suppliers of the reporting organization ( GHG Protocol, 2013 ). 
Upstream value chain (also referred to as "supply chain"): the activities, sites, resources, relationships, and stakeholders that provide products and/or services to your organization. This typically involves activities early in the value chain, such as production or development. The upstream value chain varies depending on the nature of the business but may include raw material, component, or equipment suppliers (adapted from ESRS, 2023 ). 
Value chain mapping:  the process of understanding the flow of activities, processes, and value creation within an organization. It involves identifying actors in the value chain, locating where they operate, and understanding the relationships between them (adapted from  AFi, 2024 ). 
Value chain: the entire sequence of upstream and downstream activities, sites, resources, and relationships associated with the reporting organization's operations, starting with the raw materials and extending through end-of-life management, and aimed at providing or receiving value from an organization's products and services within, upstream, or downstream of direct operations (adapted from GHG Protocol, 2013 ; ESRS, 2023 ; SBTN, 2023 ).
guidance.textKo: 이 섹션은 문항에서 사용하는 주요 용어의 의미를 설명합니다. 용어 해석은 CDP 2026 원문과 국문 양식의 용어 사용을 우선 적용하십시오.

criteria.criteria:
Points will be awarded per completed cell in proportion to the number of cells displayed (excluding column 'Smallholder inclusion in mapping').
A maximum of 5/5 points is available for this question.
criteria.criteriaKo: 이 평가기준은 해당 점수 수준에서 요구되는 조건입니다. 배점표, 문항 응답 구조, 완료 셀, 선택지 및 설명 입력 여부를 함께 확인하십시오.

criteria.criteria:
Full Disclosure points must be awarded to be eligible for Awareness points.
ROUTE A)
'Yes, we have mapped or are currently in the process of mapping our value chain' selected in column 'Value chain mapped' - 2 points
A maximum of 2/2 points is available for this route.
OR
ROUTE B)
'No, but we plan to do so within the next two years' selected in column 'Value chain mapped' - 1/2 points
A maximum of 1/2 points is available for this route.

change.q2025_text: Has your organization mapped its value chain?
change.detail_change_types: New guidance, New guidance, Change to conditional logic
change.change_summary:
Added general guidance on what to consider when identifying suppliers. Added definition "Supplier".
New guidance added to clarify 'upstream value chain' for financial services organizations.
Added guidance to clarify when an organizaiton might have a supplier tier known but not mapped.
Conditional logic fix - column is presented if any option is selected in column 1 "Value chain mapped"
change.scoring_change_summary: CC/W All sectors excluding Financial services Leadership • New Leadership criteria introduced to assess column ‘Value chain stages covered in mapping'. • Please note this change has not been applied to Financial services organizations. Criteria modified to increase ambition and incentivise organizations to map their upstream value chain, to improve identification of dependencies, impacts, risks and opportunities in an organization's indirect operations.
change.daml_summary: General: D 5/5 | A 1 or 2/2 | M 0/0 | L 1/1
change.reflected:
2026 question text applied
2026 response type/columns applied
2026 D/A/M/L point allocation linked
2026 scoring criteria linked
2025-to-2026 change summary linked
change.detail_change_typesKo: 이 섹션은 문항 응답에 필요한 추가 작성 기준을 설명합니다. 응답방법과 선택지를 함께 확인하여 작성하십시오.

sectorTagsKo: 금융 서비스, 석유 및 가스, 석탄, 일반, 항공, 자본재, 운송 OEM - EPM, 화학, 금속 및 광업, 운송 서비스, 철강, 농산물 원자재, 운송 OEM, 에너지 유틸리티 및 발전, 시멘트, 식품, 음료 & 담배, 제지 및 임업, 부동산, 건설
simpleHelpKo: 이 문항은 '귀사는 가치사슬 매핑을 하고 있습니까?'에 대한 배경 설명입니다. CDP 응답자는 보고연도 기준으로 조직의 환경 관련 현황, 리스크, 기회, 성과 또는 관리체계를 문항의 응답 구조에 맞춰 설명해야 합니다.
translationSourceKo: CDP 국문 양식 기반 국문 보정
